creator: steve@mouf.net
text:
-----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
Hash: SHA1

Hi,

Sorry for the slow follow up, but it seems to work for me. Please feel
free to close PR. Thanks so much for your work on this and for the
thorough explanation.

Steve

On 03/03/11 18:48, jkim@FreeBSD.org wrote:
> Synopsis: java/openjdk6 web browser plugin doesn't work with both WEB and IPV6 enabled
> 
> State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> State-Changed-By: jkim
> State-Changed-When: Thu Mar 3 23:27:58 UTC 2011
> State-Changed-Why: 
> It should be fixed in openjdk6-b22_1.  Please try and let me know if it works.
> 
> FYI, this problem is not related to IcedTea-Web plugin itself.  Please see:
> 
> http://diario.beerensalat.info/2008/10/12/java_and_ipv6_on_bsd.html
> 
> It is not even FreeBSD-specific issue.  In fact, it was one of the longest
> open PRs for Linux Java:
> 
> http://bugs.sun.com/bugdatabase/view_bug.do?bug_id=6342561
> 
> IcedTea proposed this patch:
> 
> http://icedtea.classpath.org/hg/icedtea6/rev/6530d2aa9076
> 
> However, Sun decided to close this PR and opened a new one, instead:
> 
> http://bugs.sun.com/bugdatabase/view_bug.do?bug_id=6882910
> 
> It was fixed by this commit:
> 
> http://hg.openjdk.java.net/jdk7/jdk7/jdk/rev/1f996198877b
> 
> I back-ported Sun's patch from OpenJDK7 for consistency.
> 
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=153301
